---
title: "API02: Rate Limiting"
subtitle: "Handling Rate Limits and Throttling Strategies"
author: "MAMBA Architecture Team"
date: "2025-08-27"
version: "1.0.0"
---

# Rate Limiting Strategies

## Overview

Rate limiting is crucial for maintaining stable API integrations. This document provides comprehensive strategies for handling rate limits across different platforms.

## Rate Limit Types

### 1. Request-Based Limits

Limits based on number of requests per time period.

```r
# Example: 100 requests per minute
# Example: 1000 requests per hour
# Example: 10000 requests per day
```

### 2. Concurrent Request Limits

Limits on simultaneous requests.

```r
# Example: Maximum 5 concurrent connections
# Example: Maximum 10 parallel requests
```

### 3. Payload-Based Limits

Limits based on data volume.

```r
# Example: 1MB per request
# Example: 100MB per hour
# Example: 1GB per day
```

## Rate Limiter Implementation

### Token Bucket Algorithm

Most flexible and commonly used algorithm.

```r
TokenBucket <- R6Class("TokenBucket",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(capacity, refill_rate) {
      private$capacity <- capacity
      private$tokens <- capacity
      private$refill_rate <- refill_rate  # tokens per second
      private$last_refill <- Sys.time()
      private$lock <- filelock::lock("~/.mamba/rate_limit.lock", timeout = 0)
    },
    
    acquire = function(tokens = 1, wait = TRUE) {
      private$refill()
      
      if (private$tokens >= tokens) {
        private$tokens <- private$tokens - tokens
        return(TRUE)
      }
      
      if (!wait) {
        return(FALSE)
      }
      
      # Calculate wait time
      tokens_needed <- tokens - private$tokens
      wait_time <- tokens_needed / private$refill_rate
      
      message(sprintf("Rate limit reached. Waiting %.2f seconds...", wait_time))
      Sys.sleep(wait_time)
      
      private$refill()
      private$tokens <- private$tokens - tokens
      return(TRUE)
    },
    
    get_available_tokens = function() {
      private$refill()
      return(private$tokens)
    },
    
    reset = function() {
      private$tokens <- private$capacity
      private$last_refill <- Sys.time()
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    capacity = NULL,
    tokens = NULL,
    refill_rate = NULL,
    last_refill = NULL,
    lock = NULL,
    
    refill = function() {
      current_time <- Sys.time()
      time_passed <- as.numeric(current_time - private$last_refill, units = "secs")
      
      tokens_to_add <- time_passed * private$refill_rate
      private$tokens <- min(private$capacity, private$tokens + tokens_to_add)
      private$last_refill <- current_time
    }
  )
)
```

### Sliding Window Rate Limiter

Precise rate limiting with sliding time windows.

```r
SlidingWindowRateLimiter <- R6Class("SlidingWindowRateLimiter",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(max_requests, window_seconds) {
      private$max_requests <- max_requests
      private$window_seconds <- window_seconds
      private$request_times <- numeric()
    },
    
    acquire = function(wait = TRUE) {
      current_time <- as.numeric(Sys.time())
      
      # Remove old requests outside window
      private$request_times <- private$request_times[
        private$request_times > (current_time - private$window_seconds)
      ]
      
      if (length(private$request_times) < private$max_requests) {
        private$request_times <- c(private$request_times, current_time)
        return(TRUE)
      }
      
      if (!wait) {
        return(FALSE)
      }
      
      # Calculate wait time
      oldest_request <- min(private$request_times)
      wait_time <- oldest_request + private$window_seconds - current_time
      
      message(sprintf("Rate limit reached. Waiting %.2f seconds...", wait_time))
      Sys.sleep(wait_time)
      
      # Try again after waiting
      return(self$acquire(wait = FALSE))
    },
    
    get_remaining_requests = function() {
      current_time <- as.numeric(Sys.time())
      private$request_times <- private$request_times[
        private$request_times > (current_time - private$window_seconds)
      ]
      return(private$max_requests - length(private$request_times))
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    max_requests = NULL,
    window_seconds = NULL,
    request_times = NULL
  )
)
```

### Adaptive Rate Limiter

Adjusts rate based on API responses.

```r
AdaptiveRateLimiter <- R6Class("AdaptiveRateLimiter",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(initial_rate = 2, min_rate = 0.5, max_rate = 10) {
      private$current_rate <- initial_rate
      private$min_rate <- min_rate
      private$max_rate <- max_rate
      private$last_request <- Sys.time()
      private$consecutive_successes <- 0
      private$consecutive_failures <- 0
    },
    
    acquire = function() {
      time_since_last <- as.numeric(Sys.time() - private$last_request, units = "secs")
      min_interval <- 1 / private$current_rate
      
      if (time_since_last < min_interval) {
        wait_time <- min_interval - time_since_last
        message(sprintf("Adaptive rate limit: waiting %.2f seconds", wait_time))
        Sys.sleep(wait_time)
      }
      
      private$last_request <- Sys.time()
      return(TRUE)
    },
    
    record_success = function() {
      private$consecutive_successes <- private$consecutive_successes + 1
      private$consecutive_failures <- 0
      
      # Increase rate after multiple successes
      if (private$consecutive_successes >= 10) {
        private$current_rate <- min(
          private$max_rate,
          private$current_rate * 1.1
        )
        private$consecutive_successes <- 0
        message(sprintf("Rate increased to %.2f requests/second", 
                       private$current_rate))
      }
    },
    
    record_rate_limit_error = function() {
      private$consecutive_failures <- private$consecutive_failures + 1
      private$consecutive_successes <- 0
      
      # Decrease rate immediately on rate limit error
      private$current_rate <- max(
        private$min_rate,
        private$current_rate * 0.5
      )
      
      message(sprintf("Rate limit hit! Reduced rate to %.2f requests/second", 
                     private$current_rate))
      
      # Back off exponentially
      backoff_time <- min(60, 2^private$consecutive_failures)
      message(sprintf("Backing off for %d seconds", backoff_time))
      Sys.sleep(backoff_time)
    },
    
    get_current_rate = function() {
      return(private$current_rate)
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    current_rate = NULL,
    min_rate = NULL,
    max_rate = NULL,
    last_request = NULL,
    consecutive_successes = NULL,
    consecutive_failures = NULL
  )
)
```

## Platform-Specific Rate Limits

### Platform Rate Limit Configuration

```r
PLATFORM_RATE_LIMITS <- list(
  CBZ = list(
    requests_per_second = 2,
    requests_per_minute = 100,
    requests_per_hour = 5000,
    concurrent_requests = 5,
    max_payload_mb = 10
  ),
  
  EBY = list(
    requests_per_second = 5,
    requests_per_day = 5000,
    concurrent_requests = 10,
    max_items_per_request = 200
  ),
  
  AMZ = list(
    requests_per_second = 10,
    burst_rate = 20,
    quota_per_hour = 3600,
    max_payload_mb = 5
  ),
  
  SHP = list(
    requests_per_second = 2,
    bucket_size = 40,  # Shopify uses leaky bucket
    cost_per_request = 1,  # Some endpoints cost more
    max_cost_per_second = 4
  )
)

get_platform_rate_limiter <- function(platform_id) {
  limits <- PLATFORM_RATE_LIMITS[[platform_id]]
  
  if (is.null(limits)) {
    warning("No rate limit configuration for platform: ", platform_id)
    # Default conservative limits
    limits <- list(
      requests_per_second = 1,
      requests_per_minute = 30
    )
  }
  
  # Create appropriate rate limiter
  if (!is.null(limits$bucket_size)) {
    # Leaky bucket for Shopify
    return(LeakyBucket$new(
      capacity = limits$bucket_size,
      leak_rate = limits$requests_per_second
    ))
  } else if (!is.null(limits$burst_rate)) {
    # Token bucket with burst for Amazon
    return(TokenBucket$new(
      capacity = limits$burst_rate,
      refill_rate = limits$requests_per_second
    ))
  } else {
    # Standard rate limiter
    return(SlidingWindowRateLimiter$new(
      max_requests = limits$requests_per_minute,
      window_seconds = 60
    ))
  }
}
```

## Request Queue Management

### Priority Queue for API Requests

```r
PriorityRequestQueue <- R6Class("PriorityRequestQueue",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(rate_limiter) {
      private$rate_limiter <- rate_limiter
      private$queue <- list()
      private$processing <- FALSE
    },
    
    enqueue = function(request, priority = 5, callback = NULL) {
      request_item <- list(
        request = request,
        priority = priority,
        callback = callback,
        timestamp = Sys.time(),
        id = uuid::UUIDgenerate()
      )
      
      # Insert based on priority
      if (length(private$queue) == 0) {
        private$queue[[1]] <- request_item
      } else {
        inserted <- FALSE
        for (i in seq_along(private$queue)) {
          if (request_item$priority > private$queue[[i]]$priority) {
            private$queue <- append(private$queue, list(request_item), i - 1)
            inserted <- TRUE
            break
          }
        }
        if (!inserted) {
          private$queue[[length(private$queue) + 1]] <- request_item
        }
      }
      
      # Start processing if not already running
      if (!private$processing) {
        self$process_queue()
      }
      
      return(request_item$id)
    },
    
    process_queue = function() {
      private$processing <- TRUE
      
      while (length(private$queue) > 0) {
        # Get next request
        request_item <- private$queue[[1]]
        private$queue[[1]] <- NULL
        
        # Wait for rate limit
        private$rate_limiter$acquire()
        
        # Execute request
        tryCatch({
          response <- private$execute_request(request_item$request)
          
          # Call callback if provided
          if (!is.null(request_item$callback)) {
            request_item$callback(response)
          }
        }, error = function(e) {
          warning("Request failed: ", e$message)
          
          # Retry logic
          if (private$should_retry(e)) {
            # Re-queue with lower priority
            self$enqueue(
              request_item$request,
              priority = request_item$priority - 1,
              callback = request_item$callback
            )
          }
        })
      }
      
      private$processing <- FALSE
    },
    
    get_queue_length = function() {
      return(length(private$queue))
    },
    
    get_queue_status = function() {
      if (length(private$queue) == 0) {
        return(data.frame())
      }
      
      do.call(rbind, lapply(private$queue, function(item) {
        data.frame(
          id = item$id,
          priority = item$priority,
          age_seconds = as.numeric(Sys.time() - item$timestamp, units = "secs"),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }))
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    rate_limiter = NULL,
    queue = NULL,
    processing = NULL,
    
    execute_request = function(request) {
      # Actual HTTP request execution
      response <- httr::VERB(
        verb = request$method,
        url = request$url,
        config = request$config,
        body = request$body,
        encode = request$encode
      )
      
      # Check for rate limit headers
      private$update_rate_limits(response)
      
      return(response)
    },
    
    update_rate_limits = function(response) {
      headers <- httr::headers(response)
      
      # Standard rate limit headers
      if (!is.null(headers$`x-ratelimit-remaining`)) {
        remaining <- as.numeric(headers$`x-ratelimit-remaining`)
        reset_time <- as.numeric(headers$`x-ratelimit-reset`)
        
        if (remaining == 0) {
          wait_time <- reset_time - as.numeric(Sys.time())
          if (wait_time > 0) {
            message(sprintf("Rate limit exhausted. Waiting %d seconds until reset.",
                          ceiling(wait_time)))
            Sys.sleep(wait_time)
          }
        }
      }
      
      # Retry-After header
      if (!is.null(headers$`retry-after`)) {
        wait_time <- as.numeric(headers$`retry-after`)
        message(sprintf("Server requested retry after %d seconds", wait_time))
        Sys.sleep(wait_time)
      }
    },
    
    should_retry = function(error) {
      # Retry on rate limit or temporary errors
      error_message <- tolower(error$message)
      return(grepl("rate limit|429|503|timeout", error_message))
    }
  )
)
```

## Rate Limit Monitoring

### Rate Limit Metrics Collection

```r
RateLimitMonitor <- R6Class("RateLimitMonitor",
  public = list(
    initialize = function() {
      private$metrics <- list()
      private$start_time <- Sys.time()
    },
    
    record_request = function(platform, endpoint, status, response_headers = NULL) {
      metric <- list(
        timestamp = Sys.time(),
        platform = platform,
        endpoint = endpoint,
        status = status,
        rate_limit_remaining = NULL,
        rate_limit_reset = NULL
      )
      
      # Extract rate limit headers if available
      if (!is.null(response_headers)) {
        metric$rate_limit_remaining <- response_headers$`x-ratelimit-remaining`
        metric$rate_limit_reset <- response_headers$`x-ratelimit-reset`
        metric$rate_limit_limit <- response_headers$`x-ratelimit-limit`
      }
      
      private$metrics[[length(private$metrics) + 1]] <- metric
      
      # Check if approaching limits
      if (!is.null(metric$rate_limit_remaining)) {
        remaining <- as.numeric(metric$rate_limit_remaining)
        limit <- as.numeric(metric$rate_limit_limit)
        
        if (remaining < limit * 0.1) {
          warning(sprintf("Low rate limit for %s: %d/%d remaining",
                        platform, remaining, limit))
        }
      }
    },
    
    get_statistics = function(platform = NULL, time_window = 3600) {
      recent_metrics <- private$get_recent_metrics(time_window)
      
      if (!is.null(platform)) {
        recent_metrics <- Filter(function(m) m$platform == platform, recent_metrics)
      }
      
      if (length(recent_metrics) == 0) {
        return(list())
      }
      
      # Calculate statistics
      total_requests <- length(recent_metrics)
      success_rate <- sum(sapply(recent_metrics, function(m) m$status == 200)) / total_requests
      
      # Rate limit utilization
      rate_limit_hits <- sum(sapply(recent_metrics, function(m) m$status == 429))
      
      # Requests per second
      time_span <- as.numeric(Sys.time() - min(sapply(recent_metrics, function(m) m$timestamp)), units = "secs")
      rps <- total_requests / max(1, time_span)
      
      return(list(
        total_requests = total_requests,
        success_rate = success_rate,
        rate_limit_hits = rate_limit_hits,
        requests_per_second = rps,
        time_window_seconds = time_window
      ))
    },
    
    generate_report = function() {
      platforms <- unique(sapply(private$metrics, function(m) m$platform))
      
      report <- lapply(platforms, function(platform) {
        stats <- self$get_statistics(platform)
        
        data.frame(
          platform = platform,
          total_requests = stats$total_requests,
          success_rate = sprintf("%.1f%%", stats$success_rate * 100),
          rate_limit_hits = stats$rate_limit_hits,
          avg_rps = round(stats$requests_per_second, 2),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      })
      
      return(do.call(rbind, report))
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    metrics = NULL,
    start_time = NULL,
    
    get_recent_metrics = function(seconds) {
      cutoff_time <- Sys.time() - seconds
      Filter(function(m) m$timestamp > cutoff_time, private$metrics)
    }
  )
)
```

## Batch Processing Strategies

### Batch Request Processor

```r
BatchProcessor <- R6Class("BatchProcessor",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(rate_limiter, batch_size = 100, max_concurrent = 5) {
      private$rate_limiter <- rate_limiter
      private$batch_size <- batch_size
      private$max_concurrent <- max_concurrent
    },
    
    process_items = function(items, process_fn, progress = TRUE) {
      batches <- private$create_batches(items)
      results <- list()
      
      if (progress) {
        pb <- progress::progress_bar$new(
          total = length(batches),
          format = "Processing [:bar] :percent ETA: :eta"
        )
      }
      
      for (batch in batches) {
        # Rate limit before each batch
        private$rate_limiter$acquire()
        
        # Process batch
        batch_results <- private$process_batch(batch, process_fn)
        results <- c(results, batch_results)
        
        if (progress) pb$tick()
      }
      
      return(results)
    },
    
    process_parallel = function(items, process_fn) {
      # Use future for parallel processing with rate limiting
      future::plan(future::multisession, workers = private$max_concurrent)
      
      # Create rate-limited wrapper
      rate_limited_fn <- function(item) {
        private$rate_limiter$acquire()
        return(process_fn(item))
      }
      
      results <- future.apply::future_lapply(
        items,
        rate_limited_fn,
        future.seed = TRUE
      )
      
      return(results)
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    rate_limiter = NULL,
    batch_size = NULL,
    max_concurrent = NULL,
    
    create_batches = function(items) {
      n <- length(items)
      batch_count <- ceiling(n / private$batch_size)
      
      batches <- vector("list", batch_count)
      for (i in seq_len(batch_count)) {
        start_idx <- (i - 1) * private$batch_size + 1
        end_idx <- min(i * private$batch_size, n)
        batches[[i]] <- items[start_idx:end_idx]
      }
      
      return(batches)
    },
    
    process_batch = function(batch, process_fn) {
      lapply(batch, process_fn)
    }
  )
)
```

## Best Practices

### 1. Respect Rate Limits

Always check and respect platform rate limits:

```r
check_rate_limit_headers <- function(response) {
  headers <- httr::headers(response)
  
  rate_limit_info <- list(
    limit = headers$`x-ratelimit-limit`,
    remaining = headers$`x-ratelimit-remaining`,
    reset = headers$`x-ratelimit-reset`
  )
  
  # Log rate limit status
  if (!is.null(rate_limit_info$remaining)) {
    percentage_used <- 1 - (as.numeric(rate_limit_info$remaining) / 
                           as.numeric(rate_limit_info$limit))
    
    if (percentage_used > 0.8) {
      warning("High rate limit usage: ", round(percentage_used * 100), "%")
    }
  }
  
  return(rate_limit_info)
}
```

### 2. Implement Exponential Backoff

```r
exponential_backoff <- function(attempt, base_delay = 1, max_delay = 60) {
  delay <- min(base_delay * (2^(attempt - 1)), max_delay)
  jitter <- runif(1, 0, delay * 0.1)  # Add jitter to prevent thundering herd
  total_delay <- delay + jitter
  
  message(sprintf("Backing off for %.2f seconds (attempt %d)", total_delay, attempt))
  Sys.sleep(total_delay)
}
```

### 3. Cache Responses

Reduce API calls by caching:

```r
CachedAPIClient <- R6Class("CachedAPIClient",
  public = list(
    initialize = function(rate_limiter, cache_ttl = 300) {
      private$rate_limiter <- rate_limiter
      private$cache <- new.env(hash = TRUE)
      private$cache_ttl <- cache_ttl
    },
    
    get = function(endpoint, force_refresh = FALSE) {
      cache_key <- digest::digest(endpoint)
      
      if (!force_refresh) {
        cached <- private$get_cached(cache_key)
        if (!is.null(cached)) {
          return(cached)
        }
      }
      
      # Rate limit and fetch
      private$rate_limiter$acquire()
      response <- httr::GET(endpoint)
      
      # Cache successful responses
      if (httr::status_code(response) == 200) {
        private$set_cached(cache_key, response)
      }
      
      return(response)
    }
  ),
  
  private = list(
    rate_limiter = NULL,
    cache = NULL,
    cache_ttl = NULL,
    
    get_cached = function(key) {
      entry <- private$cache[[key]]
      
      if (!is.null(entry)) {
        age <- as.numeric(Sys.time() - entry$timestamp, units = "secs")
        if (age < private$cache_ttl) {
          message("Cache hit for ", key)
          return(entry$data)
        }
      }
      
      return(NULL)
    },
    
    set_cached = function(key, data) {
      private$cache[[key]] <- list(
        data = data,
        timestamp = Sys.time()
      )
    }
  )
)
```

### 4. Monitor and Alert

Set up monitoring for rate limit issues:

```r
setup_rate_limit_monitoring <- function() {
  monitor <- RateLimitMonitor$new()
  
  # Set up periodic reporting
  later::later(function() {
    report <- monitor$generate_report()
    
    # Check for issues
    problem_platforms <- report[report$rate_limit_hits > 0, ]
    
    if (nrow(problem_platforms) > 0) {
      warning("Rate limit issues detected:")
      print(problem_platforms)
    }
    
    # Schedule next check
    setup_rate_limit_monitoring()
  }, delay = 3600)  # Check every hour
  
  return(monitor)
}
```

## Testing Rate Limiters

```r
test_rate_limiter <- function(rate_limiter, requests = 10) {
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  times <- numeric(requests)
  
  for (i in seq_len(requests)) {
    rate_limiter$acquire()
    times[i] <- as.numeric(Sys.time() - start_time, units = "secs")
    message(sprintf("Request %d at %.2f seconds", i, times[i]))
  }
  
  # Calculate actual rate
  total_time <- times[requests]
  actual_rate <- requests / total_time
  
  message(sprintf("\nCompleted %d requests in %.2f seconds", requests, total_time))
  message(sprintf("Actual rate: %.2f requests/second", actual_rate))
  
  return(list(
    times = times,
    actual_rate = actual_rate,
    total_time = total_time
  ))
}

# Test different rate limiters
test_all_rate_limiters <- function() {
  # Token bucket
  tb <- TokenBucket$new(capacity = 5, refill_rate = 2)
  message("\nTesting Token Bucket (2 req/sec, burst=5):")
  test_rate_limiter(tb, 10)
  
  # Sliding window
  sw <- SlidingWindowRateLimiter$new(max_requests = 5, window_seconds = 3)
  message("\nTesting Sliding Window (5 req/3sec):")
  test_rate_limiter(sw, 10)
  
  # Adaptive
  ar <- AdaptiveRateLimiter$new(initial_rate = 2)
  message("\nTesting Adaptive Rate Limiter (initial=2 req/sec):")
  test_rate_limiter(ar, 10)
}
```

## Related Documentation

- [API Architecture Overview](API00_architecture_overview.qmd)
- [Authentication Patterns](API01_authentication_patterns.qmd)
- [Error Handling](API03_error_handling.qmd)
- [Platform APIs](platforms/)
